title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, the ideal of $H$-free graphs has the Erdős-Hajnal property.
context_text: An ideal $I$ has the Erdős-Hajnal property if there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every graph $G \in I$ has a clique or stable set of size at least $|G|^\varepsilon$. The conjecture asserts that forbidding a fixed induced subgraph $H$ forces polynomially large cliques or stable sets, far beyond the $\frac{1}{2}\log|G|$ guaranteed by Ramsey theory.
paper_contribution: Proves as a corollary (Theorem 1.3) that for every graph $H$ there exists $c > 0$ such that every graph $G$ either contains an induced subdivision of $H$ in $G$ or $\bar{G}$, or has a clique or stable set of size at least $|G|^c$, establishing the Erdős-Hajnal property for the ideal of graphs excluding induced subdivisions of $H$ in both $G$ and $\bar{G}$.
paper_title: Pure pairs. II. Excluding all subdivisions of a graph

title: Conjecture 2.1 (reformulation of Erdős-Hajnal)
statement_text: For every proper ideal $I$, there exists $c > 0$ such that every graph $G \in I$ satisfies $\omega(G)\alpha(G) \geq |G|^c$.
context_text: The authors note that every proper ideal is contained in the ideal of $H$-free graphs for some $H$, so this is an equivalent reformulation of Conjecture 1.1 (the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture). It is further shown to be equivalent to the $\varepsilon$-coherent version Conjecture 2.2 via Rödl's theorem.
paper_contribution: Shows equivalence with the classical Erdős-Hajnal formulation and verifies the conclusion for the ideal of graphs whose complement and self both exclude induced subdivisions of any fixed $H$.
notes: Explicitly labeled 'Conjecture 2.1' in the paper; presented as a reformulation of Conjecture 1.1.
paper_title: Pure pairs. II. Excluding all subdivisions of a graph

title: Conjecture 2.2 (coherent reformulation of Erdős-Hajnal)
statement_text: For every proper ideal $I$ there exist $\varepsilon > 0$ and $c > 0$ such that every $\varepsilon$-coherent graph $G \in I$ satisfies $\omega(G)\alpha(G) \geq |G|^c$.
context_text: A massed graph $(G,\mu)$ is $\varepsilon$-coherent if every vertex has mass less than $\varepsilon$, every neighbourhood has mass less than $\varepsilon$, and every pair of anticomplete sets has minimum mass less than $\varepsilon$. The paper proves that this formulation is equivalent to Conjecture 2.1 (and hence to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture) using Rödl's theorem.
paper_contribution: Establishes equivalence with Conjecture 2.1 via Rödl's theorem (Lemma 3.1), and verifies the statement for ideals defined by excluding induced subdivisions of a fixed graph in both $G$ and $\bar{G}$.
notes: Explicitly labeled 'Conjecture 2.2' in the paper; stated as equivalent to Conjecture 2.1.
paper_title: Pure pairs. II. Excluding all subdivisions of a graph

title: Conjecture 2.4
statement_text: Let $H$ be a graph and let $P$ be a forest of $H$. Then every coherent ideal contains a $P$-filleting of $H$.
context_text: The paper's main theorem (2.3) proves the statement when $P$ is a path of $H$. The authors propose this strengthening, noting it would be best possible since the conclusion fails when $P$ contains a cycle of $H$ (as coherent ideals can have arbitrarily large girth). If true, it would imply the main theorem of [8] (strong Erdős-Hajnal property for all forests) in the same way Theorem 2.3 implies the main theorem of [18].
paper_title: Pure pairs. II. Excluding all subdivisions of a graph
